flashcards.front: What problem do attention mechanisms solve in sequence processing?
flashcards.back: They enable models to focus on relevant parts of input sequences dynamically, addressing long-range dependency issues in RNNs.

flashcards.front: What key innovation did the 2017 'Attention Is All You Need' paper introduce?
flashcards.back: Transformer architecture using self-attention mechanisms instead of recurrence for sequence processing.

flashcards.front: Why are transformers more parallelizable than RNNs?
flashcards.back: They process all sequence positions simultaneously through self-attention rather than sequentially.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of positional encoding in transformers?
flashcards.back: To inject information about token positions since self-attention is position-agnostic.

flashcards.front: What does multi-head attention allow the model to do?
flashcards.back: Attend to different subspaces/relationships simultaneously from the same input.

flashcards.front: Name three key components of transformer architecture.
flashcards.back: Self-attention, positional encoding, feed-forward networks, encoder-decoder structure.

flashcards.front: How does scaled dot-product attention prevent gradient issues?
flashcards.back: Scales dot products by √d_k to control variance during softmax.

flashcards.front: What makes transformers scalable for large sequences?
flashcards.back: Self-attention's O(n²) complexity allows parallel computation across GPUs.

flashcards.front: Why are transformers better at handling long-range dependencies than LSTMs?
flashcards.back: Direct attention between any tokens regardless of distance, unlike sequential processing.

flashcards.front: What is masked attention in decoder layers?
flashcards.back: Prevents attention to future tokens during autoregressive generation.

flashcards.front: How do transformers handle variable-length inputs?
flashcards.back: Through padding masks and position encodings independent of sequence length.

flashcards.front: What's the role of residual connections in transformers?
flashcards.back: Help gradient flow and enable training deeper networks.
